verdict_reasoning: CRISP1 is a secreted epididymal glycoprotein with no transmembrane domain. It associates with the sperm surface in two populations during epididymal maturation: a loosely bound (protein D) form released by a single wash, and a tightly bound (protein E) form that persists on capacitated sperm and participates in ZP binding and gamete fusion. Both populations are restricted entirely to the germline/reproductive lineage (sperm cells). The tightly bound form has documented sperm-surface accessibility in this narrow reproductive context, but CRISP1 is not a canonical surface-anchored protein — it has no TM domain or GPI anchor. Surface presence is real but strictly tissue/cell-type restricted to sperm in the male reproductive tract.
